title: Lilim the eighteenth Angel
wikitext:
[]: Lilim, the eighteenth Angel             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Wed Apr 04, 2007 10:26 am So I was looking at my user title... Was this even stated in the show? I've watched it in full a few times, and I remember nothing about humans being the final Angel. Could someone explain this to me, please?

[]: Re: Lilim, the eighteenth Angel             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Apr 04, 2007 10:45 am Trigger's Elysium wrote: So I was looking at my user title... Was this even stated in the show? I've watched it in full a few times, and I remember nothing about humans being the final Angel. Could someone explain this to me, please?

[]: Re: Lilim, the eighteenth Angel             Quote Post by TriLink » Wed Apr 04, 2007 10:46 am This line comes up in EOE when Misato is dragging Shinji's sorry ass around the Geofront. Misato wrote: Shinji-kun, We humans come from the mother of all life ,called Lillith. We are the 18th Angel

[]: Re: Lilim, the eighteenth Angel             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Apr 04, 2007 12:29 pm Ornette wrote: Although I think Misato is using a metaphor here.

[]: Re: Lilim, the eighteenth Angel             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Wed Apr 04, 2007 1:04 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: How would that be a metaphor? The context clearly indicates all Seeds and any humans decended from them carry the distinction of being called Angels because that is what they are, messangers of the FAR.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Wed Apr 04, 2007 1:06 pm look at me, I'm Tabris, the world is gonna end. I always thought the idea that humans were angels was kind of stupid. In fact I also thought that it was stupid that Lilith was an Angel. Of course, the show disagrees with me, but meh. Of the things I care about in the show, episodes 24 25 and 26 aren't one of them.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Wed Apr 04, 2007 1:20 pm Oh noes, Evangelion is a stand alone production that doesn't bend to your preferences.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Wed Apr 04, 2007 1:23 pm Trigger's Elysium wrote: Oh noes, Evangelion is a stand alone production that doesn't bend to your preferences.

[]: Re: Lilim, the eighteenth Angel             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Apr 04, 2007 1:25 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Ornette wrote: Although I think Misato is using a metaphor here.

[]: Re: Lilim, the eighteenth Angel             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Apr 04, 2007 2:46 pm Trigger's Elysium wrote: The other Angels are possibilities of what we might have become - humans that gave up human form. (flashed image: Kaworu - Rei) How is that supposed to work, then?

[]: Re: Lilim, the eighteenth Angel             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 04, 2007 3:45 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: How would that be a metaphor? The context clearly indicates all Seeds and any humans decended from them carry the distinction of being called Angels because that is what they are, messangers of the FAR.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Wed Apr 04, 2007 4:32 pm AEF wrote: So who created the 4 billion year old spheres that contained lifeforms with memories of other planets? But that's a whole nother can of worms I don't want to rehash.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 04, 2007 5:23 pm Trigger's Elysium wrote: Oh, if you want to ask the question of what created Adam and Lilith, then they'd probably be your most concise answer. I just don't like it being used in serious discussion for anything else because it's a completely irrlevant detail that isn't even nuanced in the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Apr 04, 2007 6:21 pm Trigger's Elysium wrote: Oh, if you want to ask the question of what created Adam and Lilith, then they'd probably be your most concise answer. I just don't like it being used in serious discussion for anything else because it's a completely irrlevant detail that isn't even nuanced in the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 04, 2007 7:28 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: They contributed more to the show than just Adam and Lilith. Other important plot devices include soul transfer tech

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Apr 04, 2007 7:38 pm Adam and Lilith had to end up in the God bodies somehow. Stands to reason the uber tech used for that trick was pirated.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 04, 2007 7:49 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Adam and Lilith had to end up in the God bodies somehow.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Wed Apr 04, 2007 7:57 pm Reichu wrote: Then again, Lilim technology kind of sucks unless we have almighty aliens to copycat.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Apr 04, 2007 8:01 pm BobBQ wrote: Reichu wrote: Then again, Lilim technology kind of sucks unless we have almighty aliens to copycat.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Apr 04, 2007 8:09 pm Reichu wrote: Just curious, are you alluding to the idea that they started off as "regular people" a la Yui?
summary:
[]: Lilim, the eighteenth Angel             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Wed Apr 04, 2007 10:26 am So I was looking at my user title... Was this even stated in the show? I've watched it in full a few times, and I remember nothing about humans being the final Angel. Could someone explain this to me, please?

[]: Re: Lilim, the eighteenth Angel             Quote Post by Ornette &raquo

 you want to ask the question of what created Adam and Lilith, then they'd probably be your most concise answer. I just don't like it being used in serious discussion for anything else because it's a completely irrlevant detail that isn't even nuanced in the show.
